hypotheses: The 'ena' output in TopModule is not being generated correctly, possibly due to incorrect assignment or timing of ena bits within the always block [evidence: log_excerpt L9 'Output 'ena' has 7942 mismatches. First mismatch occurred at time 160.']

The logic for propagating enable signals (ena[0], ena[1], ena[2]) may be flawed, especially since ena is reset to 0 at the start of every clock cycle, potentially overwriting intended enable pulses [evidence: RTL L27 'ena <= 3'b000;']

The testbench expects ena to be asserted for a specific duration (likely one cycle), but the current RTL may be asserting ena for either too short or too long, or at the wrong time [evidence: testbench L160-L162, always block checks for mismatches on ena]

The q output matches the reference, suggesting the counting logic is correct, but the enable signaling (ena) is not synchronized with the reference model [evidence: log_excerpt L10 'Output 'q' has no mismatches.']
likely_failure_points: Assignment of ena <= 3'b000 at the start of the always block may be clearing ena before the enable pulses are observed by the testbench [evidence: RTL L27]
ena[0], ena[1], ena[2] are only set within conditional branches, and may not persist for a full clock cycle as expected by the testbench [evidence: RTL L32, L41, L51]

The enable logic is tightly coupled to the q digit update logic, which may cause enable signals to be asserted in the same cycle as the digit rolls over, rather than in the following cycle as the testbench expects [evidence: testbench L155-L162, RTL L30-L55]
recommended_probes: Add probes to observe ena[0], ena[1], ena[2] in both DUT and reference at and after rollover events (e.g., when q[3:0], q[7:4], q[11:8] == 9)
Probe the value of q and ena at every clock edge, especially at time 160 and immediately before/after, to see if ena pulses align with the reference
Add a probe to check if ena is ever asserted for more than one cycle, or if it is missed entirely at rollover points
analysis_notes: The evidence is strong that the issue is isolated to the ena output, as q matches the reference and the testbench reports no mismatches for q. The first mismatch for ena occurs at time 160, which is shortly after reset is deasserted (reset goes low at time 15, then high at 50, then low at 65, but at 160 both clk and reset are low per waveform excerpt). The RTL always block resets ena to 0 at the start of every clock cycle, and only sets ena bits within conditional branches. This pattern can cause ena to be asserted for less than a full clock cycle, or at the wrong time, depending on the order of assignments and the testbench's sampling. The testbench appears to sample ena at both posedge and negedge clk, so any enable pulse that is too short or not aligned with the reference will be detected as a mismatch. There is only one attempt in the history, so no repeated signatures or stale patch strategies are present. The analysis is limited by the lack of direct signal traces for ena and q, but the log and code structure provide sufficient evidence to focus on ena assignment and timing.
